mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following a rate cut, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series diverges, or quarterly forward returns are negative in >50% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, creating short-term positive price pressure on equity indices over the following week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no significant positive correlation with industrial production growth during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the 3.5% threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of high-inflation months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable unemployment indicates a resilient labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative quarterly returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth confirms macroeconomic expansion, reinforcing corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters with positive GDP growth, or the signal exhibits zero predictive power across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals a contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain stress, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >55% of months following a >1.0% industrial production decline, or the threshold fails to predict return direction with statistical significance.
